description_long: This is a stone bridge with three prominent arches supporting its structure, reminiscent of classical masonry bridges found in historic urban environments. The bridge deck is paved with rectangular stone tiles, some of which are covered in patches of moss or weathering, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The parapet consists of a solid stone barrier with a crenellated or blocky top edge, providing both safety and a decorative touch. The arches are constructed from brickwork, with visible mortar lines and a slightly irregular, aged appearance. The supporting piers are thick and robust, ensuring stability and strength for the span. The overall coloration is a mix of gray, greenish moss, and reddish-brown brick, giving the bridge a weathered, timeworn character.
description: A weathered stone and brick arch bridge with three arches, mossy stone tiles, and a crenellated parapet. The structure exudes a historic, classical style with visible aging and robust support piers.
description_view_0: End-on view showing the solid stone parapet and tiled deck, with the bridge's width and blocky edge details visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the three large brick arches, thick piers, and the full length of the mossy stone deck.
description_view_2: Opposite end-on view similar to view 0, highlighting the parapet and tiled surface from the other side.
description_view_3: Side view mirroring view 1, displaying the arches, piers, and the crenellated parapet along the bridge's length.
attribute_railing_design: solid stone parapet with crenellated block top
